question: [File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve / transferFrom] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (approved spender) under PRECONDITIONS (victim holds USDS and has set a nonzero allowance for attacker; attacker monitors mempool for victim's approve(spender, newAmount) tx) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim calls approve(attacker, oldAmount); 2. attacker front-runs victim's approve(attacker, newAmount) by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, oldAmount); 3. victim's approve(attacker, newAmount) lands; 4. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, newAmount)), violating INVARIANT (attacker's total USDS received must not exceed the maximum of oldAmount and newAmount; _allowances[victim][attacker] must reflect only the currently authorized amount), causing scoped impact: attacker drains oldAmount + newAmount of USDS from victim, reducing victim's balance below what they intended to authorize, preventing victim from making a StabilityPool deposit and earning proportional SABLE/BNB rewards that the attacker can now claim with a larger relative share? Local proof idea: add unit test deploying USDSToken with mock core contracts, mint 200e18 USDS to victim, victim approves attacker for 100e18, attacker calls transferFrom for 100e18, victim approves attacker for 50e18, attacker calls transferFrom for 50e18, assert attacker received 150e18 and victim's balance is 50e18 instead of the intended 100e18.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit / transferFrom] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (mempool observer) under PRECONDITIONS (victim signs a permit(victim, spender, amount, deadline, v, r, s) for a legitimate protocol integration; attacker sees the signed permit in the mempool before it is included) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker front-runs by calling permit(victim, attacker, amount, deadline, v, r, s) with the same signature but spender=attacker; wait - permit encodes spender in the digest so attacker cannot change spender; instead: attacker calls permit(victim, legitimateSpender, amount, deadline, v, r, s) to consume the nonce, then immediately calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount) using the allowance just set for legitimateSpender - but attacker is not legitimateSpender so this fails; REVISED: attacker who IS the intended spender calls permit then transferFrom before victim's intended use), violating INVARIANT (_nonces[owner] must increment on each permit call preventing replay; allowance set by permit must only be usable once per nonce), causing scoped impact: attacker uses a valid permit signature to set allowance and drain victim's USDS before victim can deposit to StabilityPool, denying victim reward-earning position? Local proof idea: add unit test where victim signs permit for attacker as spender with amount=100e18, attacker calls permit then transferFrom in same block, assert victim's balance is zero and attacker received 100e18, and a second permit call with same (v,r,s) reverts due to nonce mismatch.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim has signed a permit with deadline=type(uint256).max and amount=type(uint256).max, intending it for a single protocol interaction; the signed permit is broadcast or leaked) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls permit(victim, attacker, type(uint256).max, type(uint256).max, v, r, s) to set infinite allowance; 2. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, victim_balance) at any future time), violating INVARIANT (a permit signature must not grant perpetual unlimited spending rights beyond the intended single interaction; allowance must not persist beyond the intended use), causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's entire USDS balance at any future time, including USDS earned from borrowing or received as rewards, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: fuzz test with arbitrary (amount, deadline) permit parameters, assert that after permit is consumed via transferFrom the allowance is zero and no further transferFrom succeeds without a new permit.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: increaseAllowance / transferFrom] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER (approved spender) under PRECONDITIONS (victim has existing nonzero allowance for attacker; victim calls increaseAllowance(attacker, addedValue) to add more) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker monitors mempool for victim's increaseAllowance tx; 2. attacker front-runs by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, currentAllowance) to drain existing allowance; 3. victim's increaseAllowance lands setting allowance to addedValue; 4. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, addedValue)), violating INVARIANT (total USDS transferred via transferFrom must not exceed the maximum single authorized amount; increaseAllowance must not create a double-spend window), causing scoped impact: attacker drains currentAllowance + addedValue from victim, preventing victim from depositing intended USDS to StabilityPool and earning SABLE/BNB rewards? Local proof idea: unit test with victim balance 300e18, initial allowance 100e18, attacker drains 100e18, victim increaseAllowance by 50e18, attacker drains 50e18, assert attacker total is 150e18.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve / permit cross-interaction] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim has an existing permit-set allowance for attacker; victim calls approve(attacker, newAmount) to change it) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker sees victim's approve tx in mempool; 2. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, permitAllowance) draining the permit-set allowance; 3. victim's approve(attacker, newAmount) lands; 4. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, newAmount)), violating INVARIANT (the combination of permit and approve must not allow a spender to consume both the permit-set allowance and the approve-set allowance in sequence), causing scoped impact: attacker drains permitAllowance + newAmount from victim's USDS balance, gaining excess USDS value? Local proof idea: unit test where victim first uses permit to set allowance 100e18, then calls approve(attacker, 50e18), attacker drains both in sequence, assert total drained is 150e18.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: transfer] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (_requireValidRecipient only blocks stabilityPoolAddress, troveManagerAddress, borrowerOperationsAddress but NOT DefaultPool, CollSurplusPool, GasPool, SableStakingV2, or CommunityIssuance) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transfer(defaultPoolAddress, amount) or transfer(gasPoolAddress, amount) or transfer(communityIssuanceAddress, amount) or transfer(sableStakingAddress, amount)), violating INVARIANT (USDS must not be transferable to core protocol contracts that have no mechanism to recover or account for directly-transferred USDS; totalUSDSDeposits in StabilityPool must equal actual USDS token balance of StabilityPool), causing scoped impact: USDS sent to unprotected core contracts is permanently locked, reducing circulating USDS supply and distorting reward accounting? Local proof idea: unit test calling transfer(defaultPoolAddress, 1e18) and transfer(gasPoolAddress, 1e18), assert both succeed and USDS is irrecoverably locked in those contracts.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: transferFrom] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has allowance from victim; _requireValidRecipient does not block DefaultPool, CollSurplusPool, GasPool, SableStakingV2, CommunityIssuance) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transferFrom(victim, communityIssuanceAddress, amount) or transferFrom(victim, sableStakingAddress, amount)), violating INVARIANT (USDS must not be depositable into CommunityIssuance or SableStakingV2 via direct transferFrom since those contracts have no USDS accounting; victim's USDS must not be redirected to unintended contracts), causing scoped impact: victim's USDS is locked in an unprotected core contract, victim loses USDS and cannot deposit to StabilityPool to earn rewards? Local proof idea: unit test where attacker has allowance, calls transferFrom(victim, communityIssuanceAddress, amount), assert USDS balance of communityIssuanceAddress increases and victim cannot recover it.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + transferFrom cross-module: StabilityPool.provideToSP] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim intends to call StabilityPool.provideToSP(amount, frontEnd); victim has USDS balance >= amount; attacker has existing allowance from victim) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker monitors mempool for victim's provideToSP tx; 2. attacker front-runs by calling transferFrom(victim, attacker, victim_balance) to drain victim's USDS; 3. victim's provideToSP call reverts because sendToPool fails due to insufficient balance), violating INVARIANT (a victim's intended StabilityPool deposit must not be preventable by an approved spender draining their balance; reward eligibility must not be manipulable by allowance exploitation), causing scoped impact: attacker prevents victim from entering StabilityPool before a SABLE issuance event, attacker deposits instead and claims the SABLE/BNB rewards the victim would have earned? Local proof idea: integration test with StabilityPool mock, victim has 1000e18 USDS and allowance set for attacker, attacker drains before provideToSP, assert victim's deposit is 0 and attacker's deposit captures the reward epoch.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit + StabilityPool.provideToSP cross-module] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim signs a permit for StabilityPool to spend their USDS for a deposit; attacker observes the permit signature in the mempool) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls permit(victim, attacker, amount, deadline, v, r, s) - FAILS because digest encodes spender=stabilityPool not attacker; REVISED: attacker calls permit(victim, stabilityPool, amount, deadline, v, r, s) consuming the nonce; 2. victim's provideToSP tx arrives but the permit call within it fails due to consumed nonce; 3. attacker who already has a separate allowance calls transferFrom to drain), violating INVARIANT (permit nonce consumption must not be weaponizable to block a victim's intended StabilityPool deposit while enabling attacker to drain), causing scoped impact: victim's deposit is blocked, attacker drains USDS and deposits it themselves to capture SABLE/BNB rewards? Local proof idea: unit test where attacker front-runs permit call consuming nonce, victim's subsequent permit-based deposit reverts, assert victim has zero SP deposit.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: sendToPool] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (sendToPool is callable only by stabilityPoolAddress; _poolAddress parameter is not validated to equal stabilityPoolAddress) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. if StabilityPool has a bug allowing arbitrary _poolAddress in _sendUSDStoStabilityPool, attacker triggers provideToSP with a crafted call path; 2. sendToPool(_sender, attackerControlledAddress, _amount) moves USDS from _sender to attacker's address), violating INVARIANT (sendToPool must only move USDS to the StabilityPool contract itself; USDS moved via sendToPool must always increase StabilityPool's token balance by exactly _amount), causing scoped impact: USDS is redirected from depositor to attacker-controlled address, attacker receives USDS without providing deposit, gaining reward-bearing position without entitlement? Local proof idea: unit test calling sendToPool from a mock StabilityPool with _poolAddress set to attacker address, assert USDS moves to attacker and StabilityPool balance is unchanged.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: returnFromPool] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (returnFromPool is callable by troveManagerAddress OR stabilityPoolAddress; _receiver parameter has no _requireValidRecipient check; _poolAddress is not validated) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. if StabilityPool has a reentrancy or logic bug that allows attacker to influence _receiver in _sendUSDSToDepositor; 2. returnFromPool(stabilityPoolAddress, attackerAddress, amount) moves USDS from StabilityPool to attacker), violating INVARIANT (returnFromPool must only send USDS to the legitimate depositor or redemption recipient; USDS returned from pool must reduce StabilityPool balance by exactly the compounded deposit amount), causing scoped impact: attacker receives USDS from StabilityPool without having a valid deposit, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: unit test calling returnFromPool from mock TroveManager with _receiver=attacker and _poolAddress=stabilityPool, assert USDS moves to attacker and StabilityPool balance decreases.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: returnFromPool - no _requireValidRecipient] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (TroveManager calls returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, liquidator, gasCompensation) in _sendGasCompensation; _receiver is not validated against the blacklist) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. if TroveManager's _sendGasCompensation is called with a manipulated liquidator address that is a core contract; 2. returnFromPool(gasPoolAddress, stabilityPoolAddress, amount) would move USDS from GasPool to StabilityPool without proper accounting), violating INVARIANT (USDS returned from GasPool must go to the legitimate liquidator; StabilityPool.totalUSDSDeposits must equal its actual USDS token balance), causing scoped impact: USDS is sent to StabilityPool without updating totalUSDSDeposits, creating an accounting surplus that future depositors can claim as excess rewards? Local proof idea: unit test calling returnFromPool(gasPool, stabilityPool, amount) from mock TroveManager, assert StabilityPool token balance increases but totalUSDSDeposits is unchanged, creating extractable surplus.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: burn] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (burn is callable by borrowerOperationsAddress, troveManagerAddress, or stabilityPoolAddress; _account parameter is any address; no consent check from _account) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. if StabilityPool has a bug where offset() can be called with a crafted _debtToOffset that causes burn(victimAddress, amount) instead of burn(address(this), amount); 2. victim's USDS balance is burned without compensation), violating INVARIANT (burn must only reduce USDS from accounts that have validly incurred debt or made deposits; a victim's USDS must not be burned without corresponding debt reduction or deposit offset), causing scoped impact: victim loses USDS balance without debt reduction, cannot repay trove or deposit to StabilityPool, losing reward eligibility? Local proof idea: unit test calling burn(victim, amount) from mock StabilityPool, assert victim's balance decreases and totalSupply decreases, with no corresponding debt or deposit reduction.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit - ecrecover zero address] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (ecrecover returns address(0) for malformed signatures in Solidity 0.6.11; permit checks require(recoveredAddress == owner) but does not explicitly check recoveredAddress != address(0)) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls permit(address(0), spender, amount, deadline, v=0, r=0, s=0) with a signature that causes ecrecover to return address(0); 2. recoveredAddress == owner evaluates to address(0) == address(0) = true; 3. _approve(address(0), spender, amount) is called), violating INVARIANT (_approve asserts owner != address(0) so this should revert; but test whether the assert fires before or after the allowance is set), causing scoped impact: if assert does not fire, spender gains allowance over address(0)'s balance (which is 0), no direct fund loss but tests the zero-address permit path? Local proof idea: unit test calling permit(address(0), spender, 0, maxUint, 0, 0, 0), assert it reverts due to assert(owner != address(0)) in _approve.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit - signature malleability] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (secp256k1 ECDSA signatures have two valid (r, s) pairs for any message; Solidity's ecrecover accepts both high-s and low-s values; permit does not enforce low-s) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim signs permit(owner, spender, amount, deadline, v, r, s); 2. attacker computes the complementary signature (v', r, s') where s' = secp256k1_order - s; 3. attacker calls permit(owner, spender, amount, deadline, v', r, s') before victim's tx), violating INVARIANT (each permit signature must be usable exactly once; nonce must prevent replay of any equivalent signature), causing scoped impact: attacker consumes victim's nonce with the malleated signature, blocking victim's intended permit-based deposit or approval, potentially enabling attacker to drain via a separate allowance? Local proof idea: unit test computing malleated (v, s) values for a valid permit signature, call permit with malleated values, assert it succeeds and increments nonce, then assert original signature fails.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve + StabilityPool.withdrawFromSP cross-module] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (victim is a StabilityPool depositor with accumulated SABLE/BNB gains; victim calls withdrawFromSP which internally calls returnFromPool to send USDS back; attacker has allowance on victim's address) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim calls withdrawFromSP(amount, priceFeedData); 2. returnFromPool sends USDS to victim; 3. attacker immediately calls transferFrom(victim, attacker, amount) in the same block after withdrawal), violating INVARIANT (USDS returned to a depositor after withdrawal must remain under their control; an approved spender must not be able to drain withdrawn USDS before the depositor can use it), causing scoped impact: attacker drains victim's withdrawn USDS including the principal they deposited, gaining excess value from the protocol? Local proof idea: integration test where victim withdraws from SP, attacker has pre-set allowance and drains in same block, assert attacker received victim's withdrawn USDS.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve race + StabilityPool reward snapshot timing] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (a SABLE issuance event is pending; victim has USDS and intends to call provideToSP to capture the issuance; attacker has existing allowance on victim) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker monitors for pending SABLE issuance; 2. attacker front-runs victim's provideToSP by draining victim's USDS via transferFrom; 3. attacker calls provideToSP with their own USDS or the drained USDS; 4. SABLE issuance is triggered and attacker captures a larger share), violating INVARIANT (SABLE rewards must be distributed proportionally to legitimate depositors; an attacker must not be able to increase their reward share by draining a competing depositor's USDS), causing scoped impact: attacker captures SABLE rewards that should have been shared with victim, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting that for any two depositors with equal USDS, their SABLE gain after equal time is equal, and that draining one depositor's USDS before deposit does not increase the other's gain beyond their proportional share.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: transferFrom - order of operations] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (transferFrom executes _transfer before _approve/allowance deduction; if _transfer triggers a callback in recipient) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker sets recipient to a contract with a receive/fallback that calls transferFrom again; 2. first transferFrom call executes _transfer moving tokens; 3. recipient callback calls transferFrom again before allowance is decremented; 4. second transferFrom executes _transfer again), violating INVARIANT (total USDS transferred via transferFrom must not exceed the approved allowance; reentrancy through recipient callback must not enable double-spend of allowance), causing scoped impact: attacker drains 2x the approved allowance from victim, gaining excess USDS? Local proof idea: unit test with a reentrant recipient contract that calls transferFrom again in its fallback, assert that the second transferFrom reverts due to insufficient allowance or balance.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit - deadline boundary] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (permit uses require(deadline >= now) with Solidity 0.6.11's now alias for block.timestamp; a permit with deadline == block.timestamp is valid) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim signs a permit with deadline = currentBlock.timestamp intending it to expire immediately; 2. attacker includes the permit call in the same block as the victim's signature; 3. permit succeeds because deadline >= now is satisfied at the exact timestamp), violating INVARIANT (a permit with deadline equal to the current block timestamp should be considered expired or at minimum not replayable in future blocks), causing scoped impact: attacker uses a permit the victim believed was expired to set allowance and drain USDS, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: unit test setting block.timestamp to T, signing permit with deadline=T, calling permit in same block, assert it succeeds and allowance is set.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: domainSeparator / permit - chain fork replay] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (USDSToken caches _CACHED_DOMAIN_SEPARATOR at deployment; domainSeparator() returns cached value when _chainID() == _CACHED_CHAIN_ID; on a BSC chain fork the chainId may remain the same) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. victim signs a permit on the original BSC chain; 2. BSC forks creating a new chain with the same chainId; 3. attacker replays the permit signature on the forked chain), violating INVARIANT (permit signatures must not be replayable across chain forks; domain separator must uniquely identify the chain and contract), causing scoped impact: attacker replays victim's permit on forked chain to set allowance and drain USDS, gaining excess value on the forked chain? Local proof idea: differential test deploying USDSToken on two chains with same chainId, assert that a permit signed on chain A is accepted on chain B.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: approve / transferFrom - fuzz allowance accounting] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (arbitrary sequence of approve, increaseAllowance, decreaseAllowance, transferFrom calls) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (fuzz: any sequence of allowance-modifying and transferFrom calls), violating INVARIANT (sum of all transferFrom amounts for a given (owner, spender) pair must never exceed the maximum allowance ever set for that pair in any single approve/permit call; _allowances[owner][spender] must always equal the remaining authorized amount), causing scoped impact: attacker transfers more USDS than authorized, gaining excess reward-bearing USDS? Local proof idea: Echidna/Foundry fuzz test with arbitrary (approve_amount, transferFrom_amount_1, transferFrom_amount_2) sequences, assert total transferred <= max single approved amount.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: _mint / totalSupply invariant] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (mint is callable only by borrowerOperationsAddress; _mint increases both _totalSupply and _balances[account]) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls mint(attacker, amount) directly), violating INVARIANT (mint must only be callable by BorrowerOperations; _totalSupply must equal sum of all _balances; every USDS mint must correspond to a valid trove debt increase), causing scoped impact: attacker mints USDS without incurring debt, deposits to StabilityPool, and claims SABLE/BNB rewards without valid collateral backing? Local proof idea: unit test calling mint from non-BorrowerOperations address, assert it reverts with 'USDSToken: Caller is not BorrowerOperations'.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: totalSupply invariant across mint/burn] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (any sequence of mint, burn, transfer, sendToPool, returnFromPool operations) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (fuzz: arbitrary sequence of all state-changing USDSToken functions), violating INVARIANT (_totalSupply must always equal sum of all _balances[address] for all addresses; no operation must create or destroy USDS without corresponding _totalSupply adjustment), causing scoped impact: accounting mismatch between totalSupply and actual balances allows attacker to claim more USDS than exists, gaining excess reward value? Local proof idea: invariant test asserting _totalSupply == sum(_balances) after every operation sequence.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: transfer / transferFrom - self-transfer] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker calls transfer(attacker, amount) or transferFrom(attacker, attacker, amount)) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls transfer(attacker, amount); 2. _transfer(attacker, attacker, amount) executes: _balances[attacker] -= amount then _balances[attacker] += amount), violating INVARIANT (self-transfer must not change any balance; _balances[attacker] must be identical before and after a self-transfer), causing scoped impact: if SafeMath ordering causes intermediate underflow or if balance is double-counted, attacker gains excess USDS? Local proof idea: unit test calling transfer(self, balance) and assert balance is unchanged and no revert occurs.

[File: 'File Name: src/USDSToken.sol -> Scope: Critical Reward extraction or unfair reward access: any bug that lets a user access rewards they should not receive, enter or manipulate state before rewards are distributed, bypass eligibility or timing rules, claim more rewards than other users with the same entitlement, repeat/replay reward claims, or otherwise gain excess reward value from the protocol'] [Function: permit - nonce replay prevention] Can an unprivileged ATTACKER under PRECONDITIONS (attacker has obtained a valid permit signature from victim; attacker calls permit once successfully) trigger CALL_SEQUENCE (1. attacker calls permit(owner, spender, amount, deadline, v, r, s) successfully; 2. attacker calls permit(owner, spender, amount, deadline, v, r, s) again with the same parameters), violating INVARIANT (_nonces[owner] must increment after each successful permit call; a permit signature must be usable exactly once), causing scoped impact: if nonce is not properly incremented, attacker replays the permit to reset allowance after it was consumed, enabling repeated draining of victim's USDS? Local proof idea: unit test calling permit twice with same parameters, assert second call reverts due to nonce mismatch.
